payload.description: Codex Stop delivery (authoring-time conformance cut-2): agnostic _run_stop orchestrator + adapter-owned Stop protocol (stop_should_check + format_stop_feedback) via Claude-Code-hook family helper; install Codex Stop hook; 9th canonical capability key; correct Codex post_tool_use_hook

payload.reason: Two independent plan reviews converged: R1 Claude subagent + Codex exec cross-review (1 BLOCKER marker + 4 SHOULD-FIX + NITs, all verified vs source and folded in; architecture — agnostic _run_stop + adapter-owned Stop protocol + family helper, no core-is-base cycle — confirmed by both). R2 focused Codex re-review: all 4 fixes CORRECT, 2 RED-text nits fixed. Verdict: ready-to-implement.

payload.suggested_fix: Re-run the failed checks locally, fix the underlying issues, then re-run `super-harness verify`.

payload.reason: Independent Claude code-reviewer subagent (5/5 pass) + Codex cross-review (IMPORTANT + 2 suggestions addressed).

findings.note: RESOLVED. Codex cross-review (IMPORTANT) flagged that the design's "LIVE-verified through the real adapter" claim rested on evidence in private/research/2026-07-01-codex-stop-livecheck.md, which is gitignored and therefore absent from the reviewable diff. Fixed in the follow-up commit by adding an in-diff "LIVE confirmation result (Task 9)" section to the tracked design doc summarizing the observed Stop Blocked -> Stop Completed sequence, the advisory reaching the model, and Codex surfacing-to-human (not self-authorizing a bypass). Full transcript remains local (private/), matching the cut-1 bite-test record convention.

RESOLVED. Added test_run_stop_systemexit_propagates_not_swallowed (tests/unit/daemon/test_hook_entry.py) locking the SystemExit-vs-except-Exception invariant: a guard that raises SystemExit(7) propagates (not rewritten to fail-open 0). Both reviewers confirmed the code was already correct (`except Exception` does not catch BaseException); this adds the missing regression lock.

Codex suggestion applied: codex.py module docstring now lists the Stop hook (was PreToolUse + SessionStart only); a half-line comment marks the in-`try` placement of `stop_should_check` as deliberate fail-open (a buggy third-party adapter guard fails open to allow rather than breaking the agent).

Non-blocking, environmental (not a code defect): the 9 integration tests in test_hook_entry_stop.py shell out to the `super-harness-hook` console script and require `.venv/bin` (or an activated editable install) on PATH — pass 9/9 with it, FileNotFoundError without. Pre-existing pattern (cut-1's Stop integration tests do the same); CI must keep the console script on PATH. No `docs/adapters/codex.md` exists, so no stale Codex capability doc.

verdict.summary: Two independent code reviews: a Claude code-reviewer subagent (all 5 checklist items PASS, adversarially verified — agnostic _run_stop with a different-guard-field stub, family-helper factoring, _CODEX_STOP_MARKER reinstall-idempotency, SystemExit fail-open interaction, import-linter core-is-base 1 kept/0 broken, mypy clean, full suite 1582 passed) and a Codex exec cross-review (request-changes on one IMPORTANT: LIVE evidence not reviewable in-diff, plus two suggestions). The IMPORTANT and both suggestions were addressed in a follow-up commit; all checklist items PASS.
